title: Power Couple? AI Growth and Renewable Energy Investment
abstract: arXiv:2603.26678v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: AI and renewable energy are increasingly framed as a "power couple," on the premise that surging AI demand will accelerate clean-energy investment, yet concerns persist that AI will entrench fossil-fuel carbon lock-in. We reconcile these views by modeling the equilibrium between AI growth and renewable investment. In a parsimonious game, a policymaker designs policies that guide investment in renewable capacity for AI, while an AI developer chooses its model's capability. The equilibrium depends on scaling regimes and market incentives. When the market payoff to capability is supermodular and performance gains are near-linear in compute (so the market rewards capability at least as fast as scaling raises its energy cost), developers push toward frontier scale even when the marginal megawatt-hour is fossil-based. In this regime, renewable expansion mainly relaxes scaling constraints rather than displacing fossil generation; clea

Title: Power Couple? AI Growth and Renewable Energy Investment
Abstract: arXiv:2603.26678v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: AI and renewable energy are increasingly framed as a "power couple," on the premise that surging AI demand will accelerate clean-energy investment, yet concerns persist that AI will entrench fossil-fuel carbon lock-in. The equilibrium depends on scaling regimes and market incentives. translated_title: 强力组合？人工智能增长与可再生能源投资
translated_abstract: 人工智能与可再生能源越来越被视作“强力组合”，其前提是激增的人工智能需求将加速清洁能源投资，但人们仍担忧人工智能会加深化石燃料的碳锁定。我们通过构建人工智能增长与可再生能源投资之间的均衡模型来调和这些观点。在一个简约的博弈中，政策制定者设计政策引导人工智能所需的可再生能源容量投资，而人工智能开发者则选择其模型的能力水平。均衡取决于规模扩展机制和市场激励。当市场对能力的回报是超模的，且性能提升在计算量上接近线性（即市场对能力的奖励至少与规模扩展提高其能源成本的速度相当）时，即使边际兆瓦时来自化石燃料，开发者仍会推动模型向前沿规模发展。在这种机制下，可再生能源扩张主要缓解了规模扩展的限制，而非替代化石燃料发电；清洁能源的扩张更多是服务于AI增长，而非推动能源转型。
tldr: 本文通过博弈模型揭示，当市场对AI能力的回报高于其能源成本增速时，可再生能源扩张主要缓解AI规模扩展的能源约束，而非替代化石燃料，从而调和了AI加速清洁能源投资与加深碳锁定的矛盾观点。
en_tdlr: This paper uses a game-theoretic model to reconcile the conflicting views that AI growth accelerates clean-energy investment versus entrenching fossil-fuel lock-in, revealing that when market returns to AI capability outpace its energy cost growth, renewable expansion primarily relaxes scaling constraints rather than displacing fossil generation.
